sourceMovie.title: The Slumber Party Massacre
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Slumber Party Massacre works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lost Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Slumber Party Massacre clicked for you, The Lost Boys (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Slumber Party Massacre?
faq.answer: Try The Lost Boys, The 'Burbs, Fright Night Part 2—each shares DNA with The Slumber Party Massacre while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Slumber Party Massacre?
faq.answer: The Slumber Party Massacre (1982) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Slumber Party Massacre part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Slumber Party Massacre Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
